entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 1 Contest Activities
entry.content:
Today i complete the "Learn about the ABAP Data Dictionary" and the 150 points where added directly to my gameboard  (no time wait).

Yesterday i complete the activity of "Devtoberfest 2024 - ABAP, Week 1, Sept. 23" and i don't see the points in mi Devtoberfest Gameboard.  So I'm wondering where are the 1200 points of the activity, i want mi points! jejeje i guess i have to wait a i little longer?

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
Welcome @gaurav95-g and @piyushgupta !

I would like to share some links with you which might be interesting and a good starting point in SAP Community:

1. Location groups in Asia Pacific region: https://community.sap.com/t5/asia-pacific/ct-p/asia-pacific > Great opportunity to connect with other local peers and get updates about latest events.

2. Devtoberfest-Group: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/gh-p/Devtoberfest > currently running with many sessions, action items, challenges about SAP content

3. Coming up soon: SAP TechEd virtual > Join the group to not miss any update! https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-teched/gh-p/SAP-TechEd-Group

entry.title: Close a company and transfer assets and liabilities, and close the other open items in sap BYD
entry.content:
Kind regards

 

Can you help me with the following: I need:

 

  1. Close a company at the accounting level
  2. Transfer open items from company A to company B, Include CxC, CxP, pending orders (customers-suppliers).

The rest of the open items will be open items in another system and we must find a way to close them in SAP so that they do not remain as pending in our system.

 

This is in SAP BYDESING

entry.title: Re: SAP Business One audit trail report
entry.content:
Hi,

You can upgrade your system to Ver 10 SP 2408 to have Audit Trail Report.

3458114 - Audit Trail report and additional settings for history / log are available - SAP for Me

Hope this helps,

SonTran

entry.title: TRD0 in standalone SAP TM - SAP EWM and SAP ECC Integration.
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

Currently in my project every change on article level is updating delivery in ECC. As it`s retail business one delivery is having a lot of articles causing hundreds of updates of delivery. On SAP TM side we only need updates on the whole delivery status like after Good Issue, not necessary when batch is picked. In current configuration TRD0 output is triggered every time delivery is updated in ECC which is stacking our queues in SAP TM - srt_moni. Any idea how to configure TRD0 better to not get that many updates, by standard ?

entry.title: Random DKIM Error "no key for signature" when sending to Microsoft email address
entry.content:
Steps to reproduce issue :

Prerequisite : Email channel in c4c is fully setup with regard to DKIM.

1. From c4c, send 10 emails to a microsoft email address

2. Check the signatures for dkim pass or failure

3. Note that some of the DKIMs fail with error "no key for
signature" (in my recent test : 2 of 10 emails had dkim failures with 8 DKIM passes)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Scenario :

Email channel is setup in C4C and DKIM is applied on our side and on SAP side.

Issue :

When I send an email from this channel in c4c to a microsoft email address, sometimes the DKIM shows as failed with "no key for signature".  For example I sent 10 emails and 2 of them fail on DKIM with this error.  This is concerning as providers are becoming stricter with DKIM it might lead to messages not reaching customers (note that the messages were received though)

I have contacted SAP on this and they are saying there is no issue on their side and that its an issue with Microsoft with a low dns lookup value and that I should contact Microsoft.  Has anyone else checked/tested this issue and found any solution to it or any advice other than to contact Microsoft?

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

entry.title: Re: Create Extension Wizard to Create SAP UI5 Extension
entry.content:
Hi @OliverGraeff - Thanks for your inputs. Was looking into possibility of using Extension project as pre-requisites to use Adaptation Project was not met.

Hi @Arushi - Pls select Adaptation Project template, post providing project name and other details including selecting version of UI5, an option will show up asking "Do you want to create an Extension Project with name "zapp.f<>.variant1" instead? It will give you the ability to create controller extensions for the synchronous views as well as the asynchronous." Here pls try selecting as Yes.

Regards,

Vipin.

You can restore default and do it again.

Hope this helps,

SonTran

entry.title: Queries and Clarifications on Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

We would like to request your assistance with the following queries regarding of Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation and its functionalities.

 

1) Could you Please confirm the deprecation timeline, if any, for Classic Forex Valuation?


2)Has SAP provided any recommendations on the timeline for Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation activation? Should it necessarily be at the beginning of the new year (e.g., January period before January month-end).
3) Since Delta logic is used in advanced valuation, does mid-year activation matter? as there will not be any delta reference, will the entire difference be considered in activation period?
4) Apart from the forex valuation item, should we also consider switching to all the others in the list at the same time? 
 
App: ‘Schedule General Ledger’ Job

Post Discounting of Long-Term Assets and Liabilities
Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation
Post Credit-Risk-Based Impairment (We do not use this)
Post B/S Reclassification
 
The functionalities that will be deprecated are:

Perform Foreign Currency Valuation
Regroup Receivables/Payables
Perform Further Valuations

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Queries and Clarifications on Advanced Foreign Curren...
entry.content:
1) Could you Please confirm the deprecation timeline, if any, for Classic Forex Valuation?

Answer - See FAQ for Advanced Valuation in Financial Accounting

When will the classic valuation runs be deprecated?
The classic valuations will be deprecated with a future SAP S/4HANA Cloud release.

A specific date is not set.  Refer to help documentation for any updates.

 

2. Has SAP provided any recommendations on the timeline for Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation activation? Should it necessarily be at the beginning of the new year (e.g., January period before January month-end).

Answer  - As per help documentation FAQ for Advanced Valuation in Financial Accounting

3. When it's recommended to activate and start using advanced valuation in financial accounting?
Answer - SAP recommends to activate and start using the jobs of advanced valuation in financial accounting at the start of the fiscal year.

4. Since Delta logic is used in advanced valuation, does mid-year activation matter? as there will not be any delta reference, will the entire difference be considered in activation period?

Answer - See Answer to 3 above

5. Apart from the forex valuation item, should we also consider switching to all the others in the list at the same time? 

Answer - As per help documentation Advanced Valuation in Financial Accounting

 The following processes are part of advanced valuation and are available as job templates in the Schedule General Ledger Jobs app:
Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation

Post B/S Reclassification

Post Credit-Risk Based Impairment

Post Discounting of Long-Term Assets and Liabilities

As you see the processes referenced are part of valuation

Review in full

See also referenced in FAQ for Advanced Valuation in Financial Accounting


What do I've to do and pay attention to when it comes to testing and activating/deactivating advanced valuation in customer systems?
In Q systems, you can activate/deactivate advanced valuation in financial accounting as often as you want, even though postings have been created using the advanced valuation in financial accounting Jobs. In P systems, you can't deactivate advanced valuation once postings have been created.

 

See Reference 

Deprecation of Apps and Job Templates in the Area of Valuation

If there are restriction notes in place, where can I find them?

Knowledge-Based Articles (KBA):

  • 3290794 - Restrictions for Advanced Valuation in Financial Accounting Jobs in SAP S/4 HANA Cloud

  • 3290742 - Restrictions for Classic Valuation Runs in SAP S/4 HANA Cloud

entry.title: Re: Updating Extension Field with External Hook in SAP Se...
entry.content:
Is it necessary to send the entire currentImage data in the update request, or is it sufficient to only send the extension field zWebSite?

The response to the external hook can be seen as an http PATCH, so you send only the fields you want to update.

So, in your case, if you only want to update "zWebSite", the payload of the response will be an application/json like this:

 

{

    "data": {

        "zWebSite": "its_value"

    }

}

 

 

Reference: https://help.sap.com/docs/CX_NG_SALES/ea5ff8b9460a43cb8765a3c07d3421fe/75a98757fa2d4117b35d81aa07f0464f.html

entry.title: Re: GP Query Report for the current month to be sent to e...
entry.content:
Hi @wiz_traceremo , there are some issues with your query.

1. There's a misplaced closing parenthesis of the function DAYS_BETWEEN.

You want to compare the DAYS_BETWEEN with 0, so you should place the =0 outside the function call, like this:

DAYS_BETWEEN(...) = 0

2. The function DAYS_BETWEEN have 2 parameters only, the two dates you are comparing:

DAYS_BETWEEN(MM, Date1, Date2) - this will not work
DAYS_BETWEEN(Date1, Date2) - this is Ok

3. If you want the invoices of the current month, you could try this:

DAYS_BETWEEN(T0.[DocDate], CURRENT_DATE) < DAYOFMONTH(CURRENT_DATE)

Don't forget to change in both SELECT statements.

entry.title: Re: Screen Personas : Change Cell Value In Table
entry.content:
As far as I understand, 

we can change the cell that are editable, 

but we cannot change it if it is a cell with a display.

entry.title: Re: mobile connectivity in mdk application access data th...
entry.content: Unless the on-premise destination is directly accessible from the internet you will need expose it to BTP using SAP Cloud Connector.   In that case the URL for the service would be the virtual host / port followed by the service path (/sap/opu/odata/SAP/ZDEMO_MDK_SRV) from the Cloud Connector and also specify the Cloud Connector Location ID in the Mobile Services destination.  In this case, the authentication is also typically set to Cloud Connector SSO to propagate the user identity to the on-premise server.

entry.title: Re: 🟣 Develop with SAP Build Apps Like a Pro
entry.content: Thanks for your session!

entry.title: Re: Not getting data and 401 Unauthorize error in Mobile...
entry.content: You mention using IAS for authentication.  Depending on your configuration it may be that the platform user you are logged in as when accessing the BTP cockpit is not the same as the application user when logging into the MDK client.   This would explain why data is returned with Open in Browser but fails with a 401 error from the client.  Even though you have specified Basic Authentication in the BTP destination the Cloud Connector may still be trying to propagate your current user identity to the backend resulting in the 401 failure when running from the MDK client.

Thanks for this insight session. It was great!

I will be thankful if you can provide some help. Thanks in advance!!!

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest 2024

entry.title: Re: How can an independent consultant purchase a new 'red...
entry.content:
Hi Marcus,

I am sorry that you are having these issues. I am however pleased to announce that we have new functionality built to help support independent consultants.

As of yesterday (23/09), once you press the Buy Now button as shown below, you will now get a question asking if you are purchasing as a company or an individual (Second screenshot).

Please select Individual and go through the remaining steps. As you started this journey before the transition, if you do run into any issues, please reach out to sales@sap.com and we would be happy to help you through the journey.

Please note at this point, the functionality only applies to SAP Learning Hub.

Sheena

Digital Sales Team

entry.title: Re: Create Extension Wizard to Create SAP UI5 Extension

entry.title: Re: Data locking in measures
entry.content: Sorry, I'm kinda new to SAC. I didn’t notice the measures in the last line; I only saw dimensions and assumed it was only for dimensions. Thanks for your answer!

entry.title: Re: Custom CDS Views - Using Multiple Data Sources
entry.content:
Hello @Mischa9i Mischa,

in order to sum up your orders, you need a way to aggregate. In the Custom CDS Views app itself, you cannot use group by as this CDS language feature is not available in the key user app. Aggregation therefore needs to happen in the runtime above. This could either be in OData (use the external API scenario and then formulate an according OData request that aggregates) or in an analytical query (use the Analytical Cube scenario and build a Custom Analytical Query on top).

Best Regards
Andreas

entry.title: What data can you use Bulk Uploads for?
entry.content:
Our business owners are interested to understand if we can use 'Bulk Uploads' for tasks which we currently carry out manually and individually for employees. One example given was the changing of hours for employees, currently we do this on a individual basis, but sometimes there can be a large number to process, can Bulk Uploads be used to do this?

If your business uses Bulk Uploads, what tasks do you use it for?

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest 2024

entry.title: Re: Setting up SAP Cloud Transport Management for SAP BTP ABAP Environment
entry.content:
Hi @Abhyudaya,

Yes, you can also use SAP Cloud Transport Management service to transport content across different global accounts. The only prerequisite is that the target account is reachable via a destination. Then, transports are possible across global accounts or even across data centers (regions).

Best regards,
Boris

entry.title: How do you utilise SuccessFactors to allow customer to completed self certification for sickness?
entry.content:
We are reaching out to the community to see if we can get a better understanding of what other SF customers do for Sickness Cerrtification and if anyone out there uses Self-Service for this.

We have over 30000+ employees and self service sickness certification is currently only available to around 5000 of those in Management / Support roles, due to the complexity of the process. We're hoping to make this process a lot more straight forward and simple to be able to roll out Self Service to the whole company. However, to do this, we'd like to understand if it's possible? What others have experienced? And any feedback we can obtain to help manage this the best way possible.

Your help / feedback would be very much appreciated from one SF customer to another.

Thanks

entry.content:
Clients run this in a desktop application, running on Windows.

I didn't find anything about OEM license.

entry.title: Difference between CIG and EDI for Ariba
entry.content: We are using a legacy B2Bi software from IBM and considering if there would be any benefits to moving to CIG as I am hearing more about it for Ariba. New to this area. Any information would help in the discovery process.

entry.title: Re: Create Extension Wizard to Create SAP UI5 Extension
entry.content:
There is no "Create Extension Wizard", you just use Adaptation project wizard and if the app does not qualify for adaptation, it will automatically create extension project for you. The SAP document that mentions "Create Extension Wizard" is misleading and should be corrected.

What is the app id you are trying to adapt? And what is your S4 version?

entry.title: Navigating Saudi Arabia’s Data Protection Framework: Part 1 – National Data Management Standards
entry.content:
What is Data Protection Compliance Framework for Saudi Arabia?

Saudi Arabia has implemented a comprehensive regulatory framework for data governance and protection, aimed at ensuring the responsible handling of both general and personal data. This framework consists of:

  1. Part 1 – National Data Management and Personal Data Protection Standards – A broader regulatory framework governing how organizations must manage data, covering various domains such as data security, data residency, and data privacy.
  2. Part2 – Personal Data Protection Law (PDPL) – A specific subset within the broader framework, focusing solely on the protection of personal data. This law establishes clear guidelines for how personal data is processed, stored, and transferred, with a particular emphasis on protecting the rights of individuals (data subjects).
  3. Part 3 – Data Residency – A critical aspect of the PDPL, which regulates the handling and transfer of personal data outside Saudi Arabia. The focus is on ensuring that personal data related to individuals within the Kingdom is safeguarded even when stored or processed in foreign jurisdictions.

Failure to comply with these regulations can result in severe penalties, legal consequences, and reputational damage. As organizations work toward the September 2024 compliance deadline, SAP provides solutions that help ensure regulatory adherence across all domains.

As we explore the structure of this regulatory framework, it's crucial to understand how compliance must be viewed from three interconnected perspectives: the broader data management standards, the specific obligations under the Personal Data Protection Law (PDPL), and the critical rules governing data residency.

Let’s begin with an overview of the National Data Management and Personal Data Protection Standards, which establish the foundational guidelines for managing data across all sectors in Saudi Arabia.

 

National Data Management and Personal Data Protection Standards: Broader Compliance Framework

The National Data Management and Personal Data Protection Standards apply to all organizations in Saudi Arabia. These standards cover 15 key domains, each addressing a specific aspect of data management and protection. Together, these domains offer a comprehensive set of guidelines that ensure compliance with data security, privacy, and residency requirements.

 

Key Areas of Data Protection under National Standards

This section outlines the critical areas that organizations must focus on to ensure comprehensive data protection across all sectors. These key areas form the foundation for broader compliance with the National Data Management and Personal Data Protection Standards:

  1. Data Classification and Categorization
    Organizations must use tools to automatically classify and categorize data based on sensitivity levels. This ensures that both personal and non-personal data are handled according to their importance and potential risk, safeguarding sensitive data from unauthorized access and breaches.
  2. Data Encryption and Security
    Data security involves more than just encryption. While encrypting sensitive data during both transit and storage is essential, organizations must also implement robust security measures that protect all data—whether personal or general—from unauthorized access, modification, or destruction.
  3. Data Residency and Storage
    Regulatory requirements dictate that organizations must store sensitive data within Saudi Arabia’s borders. This is particularly critical when utilizing cloud services, which must ensure region-specific compliance to guarantee that personal and general data are securely stored and processed locally.
  4. Data Access Controls and Privacy
    Strict access controls must be enforced using identity and access management systems. These controls ensure that only authorized personnel have access to sensitive data. Additionally, organizations must adhere to privacy policies and practices that safeguard personal data, ensuring that individuals' rights are respected.
  5. Data Retention and Deletion
    Organizations must adhere to robust data retention and deletion policies to ensure that data is stored only for as long as necessary and securely disposed of afterward. Compliance with these requirements is especially critical for sensitive and personal data.
  6. Breach Response
    Breach response is crucial given the severe penalties imposed by the Saudi government for data breaches. Organizations must have comprehensive protocols in place to effectively manage and respond to breaches. This includes notifying regulatory authorities within specified timeframes, informing affected individuals if their rights and freedoms are at risk, and mitigating any potential damage. Proper breach management also involves evaluating retained data and ensuring secure deletion where appropriate.

These key areas lay the foundation for data protection in Saudi Arabia. To ensure full compliance, it's crucial to dive into the specific requirements within each of the 15 domains. Now, let’s explore!

 

Comprehensive Overview of the 15 Domains in the Standards

Each of the 15 domains in the broader standards provides specific mandates, and SAP solutions are tailored to ensure compliance across all these areas. Below table gives a comprehensive summary:

#

Domain Name

Mandate

1

Data Governance: Ensuring Accountability and Continuous Improvement

 

Establish KPIs, continuous monitoring, and improvement for data governance, implement version control, and conduct periodic reviews for policy effectiveness.

2

Data Catalog and Metadata: Structuring Data for Compliance

Develop metadata structures and use automated tools for cataloging. Establish KPIs for regular reviews and compliance.

3

Data Quality: Ensuring Accuracy and Compliance

Implement data quality tools, monitor and address data quality issues, and establish KPIs for tracking data trends and performance.

4

Data Operations: Optimizing Performance and Security

Develop lifecycle management policies, monitor database performance, establish disaster recovery plans, and track data storage usage with KPIs.

5

Document & Content Management: Streamlining Procedures

Establish procedures for document management, implement lifecycle policies, and set KPIs to monitor document management efficiency.

6

Data Architecture & Modeling: Building a Solid Framework

Implement technology tools for data architecture, manage changes, monitor metrics, and document processes.

7

Reference & Master Data Management: Centralizing Data

Develop reference and master data management, prioritize data objects, assign Data Stewards, and document lifecycle management.

8

Business Intelligence & Analytics: Driving Strategic Decisions

Implement BI and Analytics tools, and establish KPIs to measure the effectiveness of analytics and AI portfolios.

9

Data Sharing & Interoperability: Enabling Secure Exchange

Establish secure methods for data sharing and exchange while ensuring compliance with NDMO regulations. Follow ETL/ELT processes and document activities.

10

Data Value Realization: Unlocking Business Potential

Continuously evaluate data assets for potential use cases that generate revenue or reduce costs, and establish KPIs to measure Data Value Realization.

11

Open Data: Enhancing Transparency and Accessibility

Identify, publish, and maintain open datasets, and document them in a unified register. Establish KPIs for monitoring open data usage.

12

Freedom of Information: Providing Citizen Access

Ensure Saudi citizens have access to government information and define a process for accessing this information with an appeal mechanism in case of disputes.

13

Data Classification: Enhancing Control and Compliance

Assign controls to datasets, review data classification levels, and conduct impact assessments. Document activities and establish KPIs.

14

Personal Data Protection: Safeguarding Privacy

Conduct Data Protection Assessments, establish data breach processes, and implement privacy notices and consent management for audit and compliance.

15

Data Security & Protection: Strengthening Safeguards

Implement data protection tools, establish security processes, and define minimum security rules.

 

How SAP Helps with Data Protection Compliance?

  • SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)
    • Integrates data management, privacy, and auditing capabilities with built-in DevOps tools.
    • Centralizes data and metadata, supporting data modeling and compliance.
    • Enables KPI tracking, breach detection, and automated periodic reviews.
  • SAP Governance Risk and Compliance (GRC) Solutions
    • Facilitates regulatory compliance across multiple domains, including data access controls, breach notifications, and consent management.
    • Supports real-time monitoring, access control enforcement, and compliance with global standards like ISO 27001.
    • Ensures effective data sharing and management through continuous monitoring and predictive insights.
  • SAP Analytics Cloud
    • Provides pre-built KPIs, AI-driven insights, and real-time data analysis for predictive monitoring and compliance.
    • Supports business intelligence (BI), reporting, and data visualization for strategic decision-making.
    • Drives financial performance and profitability through data value realization.
  • SAP Data Management Solutions
    • Supports lifecycle management, database performance, and disaster recovery.
    • Offers intelligent data quality tools with embedded AI for regulatory compliance.
    • Ensures transparency, accuracy, and accessibility in open data sharing.
  • SAP Integrated Document and Enterprise Content Management Solutions (ECM)
    • Enhances efficiency and streamlines document management with KPIs to monitor workflows.
    • Ensures regulatory compliance and improves productivity through document lifecycle management.
  • SAP Datasphere & SAP HANA
    • Provide secure data integration, role-based access controls, and compliance with ETL/ELT processes.
    • Ensure efficient data sharing with built-in monitoring tools and continuous compliance checks.
  • SAP Reference & Master Data Management Solutions
    • Centralizes data in a unified hub for both SAP and non-SAP systems.
    • Streamlines data governance and decision-making processes, ensuring data quality and compliance.
  • SAP Cyberthreat Monitoring & Privacy Management Tools (including UI Masking)
    • Provides robust security for sensitive information, ensuring compliance in a continuously evolving environment.
    • Monitors, detects, and responds to cyberthreats while protecting personal and sensitive data.

This ensures that organizations can comply with the NDMO’s Personal Data Protection Law by leveraging SAP’s comprehensive solutions across all domains.

Disclaimer:

The content of this blog is based on personal interpretation of the regulations and SAP solutions. It is provided for guidance and informational purposes only. For specific details about SAP products and their implementation, please consult with an SAP solution advisor or product expert.

In part 2 of this blog series, we’ll dive into the Personal Data Protection Law (PDPL) and its implications for businesses in Saudi Arabia. [Click here to read Part 2].

entry.title: Re: An ABAP question about memory leak and object instances
entry.content:
Hi Sandra

Yes I understand the syntax you suggest, but I was just wondering how garbage collection takes place in the example from SAP standard documentation:

NEW cl_sql_statement( con_ref = con_ref )->execute_ddl( create_string ).

I think the syntax suggested by SAP is bad because the object (and memory) can't be free'ed again, but maybe I'm wrong. That is what I would like to know.

entry.title: Re: Creating a custom form entry page with Flexible Programming Model
entry.content:
Hi,

it looks like the Explorer page doesn't work anymore.

Flexible Programming Model Explorer (ondemand.com)

Regards Wolfgang

entry.title: Set Kanban Container Status - Batch number
entry.content:
Hi SAP gurus,

we use Batch number in app Set Kanban Container Status. There are more possibilities how to add batch. It can be put manually / but we need to know batch number or we can scan it, or search it:

1. we want to scan batch number - our QR code format is SAPnumber_batch. How to define scanner for reading only batch ? For example: 123456_batch1 - we need to scan only batch1.

2. in search field for batch are displayed all batches for search material - how we can define that there are displayed only batches with stock quantity ?

Thanks for support 🙂

entry.title: Re: Handling GR Inbound Resource in PPDS RRP3 - Product view
entry.content:
Hi Kapoor,

Thanks for your prompt reply !!

I checked in Resource under "APO Resource" tab and yes it's Calendar Resource.

But my query is, as shown in attachment, in RRP3 - Product view, this resource appears as Goods receipt ticked "Handling Resource" and this GR (Days) and time gets added up to end time of Operation and thus leading to new End date & time. Business say, they are seeing this "Handling resource" just recently in RRP3. But in Material master i could see GR days maintained years back.

How can i make this Handling unit disappear or what might have caused this to appear to include in schedule. I can see it in Planning board graphical object as well.

Kindly advise.

Regards,

Jeffrey

entry.title: Re: Memory cleanup / reduction
entry.content: Hi Jim, We typically purge the In* tables such as INCustomer and InCustPolicy and keep 2 years of data. Additionally, there are MEssage Log tables that consume a large amount of storage that can be purged as well without any compliance issues. We can review your options and you can present them to your internal compliance teams for approval.

entry.content:
Hi,

 

Is there any APIs are available for Parking the invoice

 

Thanks

entry.title: Navigating Saudi Arabia’s Data Protection Framework: Part 2 – Personal Data Protection Law (PDPL)
entry.content:
Welcome to part 2 of the blog series Navigating Saudi Arabia’s Data Protection Framework. In this part we will focus on the Personal Data Protection Law (PDPL).

The PDPL is a subset of the broader law and specifically focuses on the protection of personal data. This law addresses how organizations must protect personal data, with special consideration for data subject rights, breach notification, and legal processing obligations.

 

Key Aspects of PDPL Compliance (Domain 14):

The PDPL establishes several key areas that organizations must focus on to ensure compliance with data protection standards. Each key aspect directly connects to the compliance mechanisms that organizations must implement, as detailed in the next section:

Data Protection Assessments

  • Organizations must assess how personal data is handled to ensure compliance with legal requirements.
  • Privacy Impact Assessments must be conducted to evaluate the risks associated with data processing activities and ensure compliance with data privacy standards.

Demonstration of Compliance

  • Organizations must demonstrate compliance by implementing:
    1. Codes of Conduct: Following best practices, including globally recognized standards like ISO 27001 for information security.
    2. Integrated Compliance: Embedding data protection policies across all layers of the organization.
    3. Policy Communication: Ensuring privacy policies are communicated and understood by all stakeholders.
    4. Certifications: While optional, certifications like ISO 27001 provide valuable evidence of compliance.

Lawful Processing & Legal Permissions

  • Clear privacy notices and explicit consent from data subjects are required before processing personal data.
  • Expanded in Lawful Processing & Legal Permissions (Section 2.4) and Roles and Responsibilities (Section 2.1).
  • Consent Management ensures that data is processed only when individuals have explicitly agreed, and privacy notices explain the purpose and methods of data processing.
  • Organizations must process personal data based on a legal framework, including:
    1. Consent: Explicit consent from the data subject.
    2. Contract: Processing necessary for fulfilling contractual obligations.
    3. Legal Obligation: Processing required to meet legal requirements.
    4. Vital Interest: Protecting the vital interests of individuals (e.g., health, safety).
    5. Public Interest: Data processing carried out for tasks in the public interest.
    6. Legitimate Interest: Processing based on legitimate interests as long as it does not override the individual’s rights.

Data Subject Rights Framework

  • PDPL enforces a structured framework for upholding data subjects' rights, including access, rectification, erasure, and more.
  • Organizations must respect and facilitate the rights of data subjects i.e. employees in the HR context, including:
    1. Right to be Informed: Individuals must be informed about how their data is processed.
    2. Right of Access: Data subjects can request access to their personal data.
    3. Right to Rectification: Individuals can request corrections to inaccurate or incomplete data.
    4. Right to Erasure (Right to be Forgotten): Data subjects can request the deletion of their personal data under certain conditions.
    5. Right to Restrict Processing: Individuals may request restrictions on the processing of their data in specific circumstances.
    6. Right to Data Portability: Data subjects can request that their data be transferred to another organization.
    7. Right to Object: Individuals can object to specific types of data processing, particularly direct marketing and automated decision-making.
    8. Automated Decision-Making and Profiling: Data subjects have the right to challenge decisions made solely by automated processes that have significant effects on them.

 

Organizational Responsibilities and Compliance Mechanisms

This section details the specific responsibilities and mechanisms that organizations must implement to meet the PDPL requirements. Each point corresponds to the Key Aspects of PDPL Compliance, ensuring a coherent flow:

Main Roles and Responsibilities: Controllers vs. Processors

  • Controllers:
    • Define the purpose and methods of data processing, hold the primary responsibility for compliance, and are liable for processor failures, sharing joint liability in the event of a data breach.
    • Define the data governance landscape, classify data (e.g., personal, sensitive), decide who processes data, and receive reports from the Data Protection Officer (DPO), data governance landscape owner and Data Processors.
    • They can be roles like CEO, COO, CHRO, Head of Legal, or CIO.
  • Breach Notification
    • Controllers are also responsible for notifying the competent authority within 72 hours of any data breach that could impact the privacy rights of data subjects. They must also notify affected individuals if the breach poses a significant risk to their rights and freedoms.
  • Processors:
    • Process data on behalf of Controllers, ensuring that their activities comply with the controller’s instructions and relevant legal permissions.
    • In an HR context, processors can include HR Admins, Payroll Admins, HRIS, and HR Business Process Owners.
    • They must ensure lawful processing based on consent, contract, legal obligation, or legitimate interest, without violating the data subject's rights.

Other Roles Accountability and Compliance Mechanisms

  • Data Protection Officer (DPO):
    • Essential for organizations engaged in large-scale data processing. DPOs are accountable for ensuring compliance with data protection regulations and maintaining oversight on individual rights.
    • DPO roles can include Chief Compliance Officer, Chief Risk Officer, Chief Audit Executive, or other legal and governance roles.
    • They perform audits (e.g., audit trails, access control), report non-compliance risks, and oversee privacy impact assessments.
  • Data Governance Landscape Owner:
    • The ownership can include Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) and Chief Data Officer.
    • Responsible for providing compliance tools such as data access controls, encryption, retention, and deletion.
  • Privacy by Design:
    • Embedding privacy into the entire lifecycle of data processing, from the initial system design to daily operations, to ensure ongoing compliance.
  • Privacy Impact Assessments:
    • Regular evaluations of data processing activities to assess the risks to individual privacy and ensure compliance with data protection laws.
  • Evidence of Compliance:
    • Organizations must track and document their compliance efforts, especially when handling breach notifications and ensuring individual rights.

 

Why the Personal Data Protection Law Matters: Key Business Drivers?

Regulatory Compliance

  • Compliance with PDPL is mandatory by September 2024, with steep penalties for violations.

Resilience Across All Data Protection Domains

  • PDPL ensures organizations adopt a robust approach to managing data throughout its lifecycle, with severe fines for breaches, including:
    • SAR 1,000,000 for data transfer violations
    • SAR 3,000,000 for publishing sensitive data
    • Up to SAR 25,000,000 for the telecom sector, with potential prison terms of up to 4 years.

Identifying Breaches in Time

  • Organizations must detect and report breaches promptly to minimize damage and legal risk.

Providing a Secure Business Environment

  • PDPL ensures that organizations can create a secure, transparent environment for managing personal data and building trust with partners and customers.

 

How SAP Helps with PDPL Compliance?

SAP solutions for Governance, Risk, and Compliance

SAP GRC support organizations in managing PDPL compliance efficiently by offering tools for:

  • Advanced Data Discovery and Classification: To ensure personal data is correctly managed and categorized.
  • Automated Breach Notifications: Pre-configured rules for quick breach notifications, helping organizations meet their 72-hour notification requirement.
  • Consent Management Frameworks: SAP helps manage and track individual consent in compliance with PDPL regulations.

Data Protection & Privacy (DPP) features in SAP solutions:

To provide more clarity, the table below maps key PDPL articles to DPP features in SAP HCM products like SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central and SAP S/4HANA HCM, showcasing how these solutions support compliance.

DPP Feature

PDPL Articles

SAP SuccessFactors

SAP S/4HANA HCM

Erase personal data when all applicable retention periods have expired.

Articles 4.4, 11.4 & 18.1 in PDPL and article 8 “Right to Request Destruction of Personal Data” in the implementing regulation of PDPL.

Data Purge

Information Lifecycle Management (ILM)

Give direct access to data subject to provide personal data

Article 10 in PDPL.

Performing Personal Data Collection

N/A

Read Audit / Log read access to sensitive personal data.

Articles 13.3 & 13.4 in PDPL and articles 20 "Disclosure of Personal Data & 33 "Records of Personal Data Processing Activities" in the implementing regulation of PDPL.

Read Audit

Read Access Logging (RAL)

Personal Information Report / Provide a retrieval function which can be used to inform the data subjects about the personal data stored about them

Articles 4.2 & 31 in PDPL and article 6 "Right to Request Access to Personal Data" in the implementing regulation of PDPL.

Personal Information Report

Information Retrieval Framework (IRF)

Consent Agreements / Provide consent for specific processing reasons

Articles 4.1, 5, 13, 15, 26 & 28 in PDPL and article 11 "Consent" in the implementing regulation of PDPL.

Consent Agreements

 

Data Blocking / The right to block unnecessary or excessive data or data that is not being processed

Article 23 in PDPL and article 26 "Processing Health Data" in the implementing regulation of PDPL.

Data Blocking

Information Lifecycle Management (ILM)

Self-Service / The right to correct incomplete, inaccurate or out-of-date data

Article 4.3 in PDPL and articles 5 "Right of Access to Personal Data" & 7 "Right to Request Correction of Personal Data" in the implementing regulation of PDPL.

Self-Service

Self-Service

The right to revoke the consent

Article 5 in PDPL and article 12 "Consent withdrawal" in the implementing regulation of PDPL.

Consent Agreements

N/A

Change Audit / Log changes to personal data

Article 22 "Correction of Personal Data" in the implementing regulation of PDPL.

Change Audit

N/A

Masking Sensitive Data  / The right to anonymize unnecessary or excessive data or data that is not being processed

Article 9 "Anonymisation" in the implementing regulation of PDPL.

Anonymization of Applications and Candidates in Recruiting

Masking Sensitive Data

SAP HANA Data Masking & Anonymization

Disclaimer:

The content of this blog is based on personal interpretation of the regulations and SAP solutions. It is provided for guidance and informational purposes only. For specific details about SAP products and their implementation, please consult with an SAP solution advisor or product expert.

In part 3 of this blog series, we’ll explore the Data Residency regulations in Saudi Arabia and how SAP helps ensure compliance with cross-border data transfers. [Click here to read Part 3].”

entry.title: Re: Error - Failed to start outlook, Internal Instance of outlook is null.
entry.content: Hello Mahara, Thanks for reaching out to us, Can you please confirm ( From the dependency section, locate Outlook SDK and include it as a dependency to your project. At the time of this tutorial creation, Outlook SDK Version 1.32.41 was used.) If you can share us the screenshot of the depency section and error screenshot will helpful for further investigation

entry.title: Re: 999 – The Number of the BSEG
entry.content:
Hello Juan Pedro,

well, I guess you successfully spotted one of the devil's details...the intercompany invoice is indeed one of the few holes we have in this story. Let me explain: as you correctly observed, the intercompany invoice uses an IDOC message type and this IDOC usually performs a so-called direct input (or a batch input session) on the FI manual postings transaction FB01. Now, for manual FI postings I explained above that we consider these "operational", we need the detailed BSEGs as "document entry" view. Also, technically the whole summarization code is just not available or processed in the FB01 transaction, so no OBCY or BADI summarization will happen here.
We know this is not nice and are already working on changing the intercompany invoice processing, so that the Accounting BAPIs (BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_CHECK/POST) will be used rather than the IDOC + batch input on FB01. Then the summarization will be processed!
What should happen in the intercompany invoice scenario, though, is an automatic accounting document split (what I call a workaround above). If the invoice contains more than 949 items, a second accounting document will be generated. So right now we only have the workaround solution (document split) in this scenario.

Muchos saludos,
Stefan

entry.title: Navigating Saudi Arabia’s Data Protection Framework: Part 3 – Data Residency
entry.content:
Welcome to part 3 of the blog series Navigating Saudi Arabia’s Data Protection Framework. In this part we will focus on the Data residency.

Data residency regulations ensure that personal data, even when transferred outside Saudi Arabia, continues to receive the highest level of protection. These regulations are aligned with the overarching goal of safeguarding the personal information of Saudi citizens and residents, wherever that data may reside.

 

Key Requirements Summary for Data Residency

Saudi Arabia's Personal Data Protection Law (PDPL) and the National Data Management and Personal Data Protection Standards place strict regulations on how personal data is handled, particularly when it comes to transferring data outside the Kingdom. The key provisions include:

  • Purpose and Necessity: Data can only be transferred or disclosed outside Saudi Arabia if it aligns with the specific purposes allowed by law, such as fulfilling agreements to which the Kingdom is a party, serving the public interest, or meeting national security objectives.
  • Minimum Necessary Transfer: Controllers must limit the transfer or disclosure of personal data to the minimum necessary and ensure that such transfers do not compromise data privacy or security.
  • Adequate Protection Measures: The level of protection in the destination country must meet or exceed Saudi Arabia's standards for data privacy, including the rights of data subjects, breach notifications, and data security.
  • Risk Assessments: Controllers are required to conduct risk assessments for data transfers outside the Kingdom, ensuring that the process is compliant with PDPL and does not introduce undue risks to data subjects.
  • Exceptions: While there are allowances for transferring data outside KSA, these are strictly regulated. Exceptions include situations where the transfer is necessary to fulfill contractual obligations, safeguard the Kingdom's vital interests, or comply with public sector requirements.

 

CCC2020 Regulations and Compliance

Public sector entities and Critical National Infrastructure (CNI) organizations, such as ARAMCO and SABIC, must adhere to the Cloud Cybersecurity Controls (CCC 2020) issued by the National Cybersecurity Authority (NCA). These regulations mandate strict data residency requirements, ensuring that data related to public sector and CNI entities remains within KSA borders. Organizations in this category must host their data in local data centers, and cloud services must be provided from within KSA.

Exemptions under the PDPL do not apply to these organizations, given their sensitive nature and the critical importance of maintaining national security and infrastructure integrity.

 

SAP’s Data Residency Compliance Plan

As part of its commitment to comply with CCC2020 regulations and ensure its customers in Saudi Arabia meet the necessary legal requirements, SAP has launched a KSA Migration Project:

  • Migration to Google Cloud Platform (GCP):
    • SAP is migrating customers to the new Google Cloud Platform (GCP) Data Center in Dammam. This data center is certified under CCC2020 regulations and provides a local, secure environment for storing and processing sensitive data.
  • Two-Phase Migration Timeline:
    • The first phase focus on migrating customers subject to CCC2020 regulations, particularly public sector and CNI organizations.
    • The second phase covers the remaining customers who are not bound by CCC2020 but still require secure local data hosting to meet PDPL compliance.
  • Enhanced Security and Compliance Features:
    • Moving SAP applications to GCP ensures the latest in cloud technology, with S/4HANA Private Cloud becoming available for Saudi customers. The migration will provide secure, state-of-the-art infrastructure compliant with both CCC2020 and PDPL.

By aligning with national regulatory frameworks and enhancing local data processing capabilities, SAP is supporting its customers in achieving compliance while leveraging cutting-edge technology.

 

Conclusion: Navigating Compliance with SAP Solutions

As Saudi Arabia strengthens its data governance through the National Data Management and Personal Data Protection Standards, PDPL, and Data Residency requirements, compliance is critical. SAP provides comprehensive solutions, including Governance Risk and Compliance (GRC) tools, SuccessFactors, S/4HANA, and Business Technology Platform (BTP), to meet these regulations effectively. These platforms offer features like Data Protection & Privacy (DPP) capabilities and other features such as breach management, consent tracking, and data retention controls.

With SAP’s integrated approach, organizations are empowered to ensure full compliance, protect personal data, and manage critical data activities while building trust and ensuring operational continuity ahead of the September 2024 deadline.

Disclaimer: 

The content of this blog is based on personal interpretation of the regulations and SAP solutions. It is provided for guidance and informational purposes only. For specific details about SAP products and their implementation, please consult with an SAP solution advisor or product expert.

entry.title: Re: Transport code of a query
entry.content:
oook, I knew there was a silly issue, I've forgot the last step, the import... I'm not used to use queries... I thought that doing the export and transporting the TR (via STMS)  would be enough.

With this I can see the new code in the infoset, but not in the report. I had to go to SQ01 an execute the query to re-generate the code of the query's report, as Sandra said.

Thank you all for your help.

entry.title: Re: Groovy or XSLT to remove XML Tag(not values) in sap c...
entry.content:
Hi Nagesh,
 
PFB the source sample payload.
 
<?xml version='1.0' encoding='UTF-8'?>
<Root1>
    <Root2>
        <ADONumber>12345</ADONumber>
        <State></State>
        <AssignedTo>SRIVASTAVA Nidhi (External)
            <nidhi.srivastava.ext@XXXXX.eu>
            </AssignedTo>
            <Priority></Priority>
            <AdditionalComments></AdditionalComments>
        </Root2>
    </Root1>
 
Thanks,
Nidhi

entry.title: Re: Process Automation: Unattended agent is disconnected automatically

entry.content:
Asking a user who is no longer active (former user) what he did 17 years ago, very optimistic?

  • In this case, it could have been better to ask a new question in Q&A,
  • BUT the question is already answered.

entry.title: How do I set a QRFC queue to status RETRY?
entry.content:
I am programming a function module that is bound to run in a QRFC queue. When the program fails, I want the queue to remain in status RETRY in order to have it run again. 

I tried to do a message E..., but then the queue goes to SYSFAIL and no retry is scheduled. 

What do I have to do in my function module to set the queue to RETRY?

entry.title: Mass Deletion of Contact Person - Customer Master Data
entry.content:
Dear colleagues,

I need to delete/remove a contact person (with a dedicated functionID) from the customer master data (XD02). This should be done massively

I tried with an LSMW but there's no way to say to the recording the position that we want to delete/remove, it change from customer to customer.

Also checked with XD99 (MASS) transaction but there's only have the option to create/update and not to delete.

Any other idea how to do it in a "functional" way?

Kind regards

Joao Pereira

entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I have developed a python script in Business Application Studio in SAP BTP and in that I am trying to consume an on premise OData service through destination.
If am giving my backend Host & port URL I am able to consume the data directly, but it would not work if I deploy it as my service is not exposed outside.
Can you please help me how to call the OData service through destination in Python Script similar to how we call in SAP UI5 applications.

Thanks & Regards,

entry.title: Re: Create Extension Wizard to Create SAP UI5 Extension
entry.content:
Hi @Arushi ,

You are right, the app F2502 does not show up in the Adaptation wizard list. 

I checked the UI software package UIS4HOP1-600 and we do have it installed, so it should really come up in the list. And I even checked in SE80 and the BSP application is indeed installed in our system, so we do have the app in our system.

So... I think either SAP did not intend for this app to  be adaptable (which can't be right, since the Fiori App library shows a section for extensibility for this app), or  SAP's just missed putting this in the list of Adaptation wizard.

I would create an SAP ticket and ask them about this.

entry.title: /SCWM/POHUITM table not updated after the quantity adjustment (O001 Process Code)
entry.content:
Hi,

I have an ODO with only 1 item inside.

I change the quantity from 10 to 12 using the Process Code O001. (With Quantity Adjustment)

Things work fine but the table /SCWM/POHUITM is not updated with the new quantity value.

Is there any PPF action missing?

Any kind of idea is appreciated.

Best Regards

entry.title: Recommended implementation of a dependent branch abroad S4 Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello experts,

My company is planning to set up a dependent branch abroad. Unfortunately, we are not sure how to implement this correctly in SAP.
Can all these points be mapped in a German company code (possibly via a ledger solution?) or does it make more sense to have different company codes?

Initial situation:
- S4 Public Cloud
- Company based in Germany
- Dependent branch office in France
- No own plant/warehouse in France
- Country-specific chart of accounts for France (mandatory)
- Own balance sheet preparation France (mandatory)
- Own tax code
- VAT in France
- Intrastat declaration
- ZM report
- Mapping of data in DE balance sheet by adding FR accounts
- Consolidation of intercompany transactions
- Drop shipment DE / (FR) / end customer

The tendency is to create a separate company code.
